mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP and consumer credit balances indicate expanding aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals labor market stabilization, reducing precautionary savings and increasing consumer spending, which lifts equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0 percent, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts reflect synchronized supply-side recovery and construction activity, which amplifies positive sentiment across cyclical sectors and pushes S&P 500 higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in at least 3 out of 4 rolling 5-year periods, or INDPRO and HOUST diverge without corresponding SPX volatility spikes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while core inflation begins to rise, real interest rates turn negative, compressing discount rates and inflating equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when real rates are negative, or the inflation-adjusted rate signal shows no statistical correlation with forward SPX returns over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reduces labor market uncertainty, supporting consumer confidence and driving positive short-term equity returns regardless of the broader business cycle phase.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero across all payroll growth deciles, or the signal reverses direction during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly increase in housing starts above 450,000 units signals robust housing demand and construction activity, which historically precedes broad economic expansion and lifts S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of months where HOUST exceeds 450,000, or the relationship breaks down when interest rates rise above 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances coupled with accelerating inflation indicate demand-pull pressures that erode real purchasing power and trigger monetary tightening expectations, which compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite simultaneous credit and inflation spikes, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 15-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production following a recessionary trough indicates capacity utilization recovery and inventory restocking, which generates immediate positive earnings revisions and drives short-term S&P 500 gains.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of post-trough INDPRO rebounds, or the signal fails to predict returns when manufacturing PMI data contradicts the index trend.
